contraindications.clinicalRationale: Safety and efficacy of initiating SGLT2i for people with an eGFR <20 ml/min per 1.73 m² are not established
contraindications.riskDescription: Unknown safety profile and efficacy in severe kidney impairment
contraindications.alternativeConsiderations: Other glucose-lowering medications may be considered based on eGFR level
contraindications.sourceSnippet: Do not initiate when eGFR <20 mL/min/1.73m²

contraindications.clinicalRationale: SGLT2i have not been studied in outcome trials of patients with T1D and are associated with high rate of diabetic ketoacidosis in this population
contraindications.alternativeConsiderations: Insulin-based therapy is preferred for type 1 diabetes
contraindications.sourceSnippet: The use of SGLT2i in type 1 diabetes (T1D) is not established and is associated with a high rate of diabetic ketoacidosis

contraindications.clinicalRationale: SGLT2i have not been adequately studied in kidney transplant recipients, who are immunosuppressed and potentially at increased risk for infections
contraindications.riskDescription: Increased risk for infections due to immunosuppression
contraindications.alternativeConsiderations: Other glucose-lowering medications should be considered
contraindications.sourceSnippet: SGLT2i have not been adequately studied in kidney transplant recipients, who may benefit from SGLT2i treatment, but are immunosuppressed and potentially at increased risk for infections

contraindications.clinicalRationale: Patients may be at greater risk for ketosis during times of prolonged fasting, surgery, or critical medical illness
contraindications.alternativeConsiderations: Temporarily withhold SGLT2i during these periods
contraindications.sourceSnippet: It is reasonable to withhold SGLT2i during times of prolonged fasting, surgery, or critical medical illness (when patients may be at greater risk for ketosis)

contraindications.clinicalRationale: Increased risk of genital mycotic infections with SGLT2i treatment in both men and women that is consistent across all trials
contraindications.alternativeConsiderations: Most infections can be managed with topical antifungal treatment
contraindications.sourceSnippet: Potential contraindications to SGLT2i initiation: genital infection risk, diabetic ketoacidosis, foot ulcers, immunosuppression

contraindications.clinicalRationale: Caution should be exercised regarding the use of SGLT2i in patients with foot ulcers due to potential increased risk of lower-limb amputation
contraindications.riskDescription: Potential increased risk of lower-limb amputation
contraindications.alternativeConsiderations: Routine preventive foot care and adequate hydration are advised
contraindications.sourceSnippet: Potential contraindications to SGLT2i initiation: genital infection risk, diabetic ketoacidosis, foot ulcers, immunosuppression

warnings.warningText: If patient is at risk for hypovolemia, consider decreasing thiazide or loop diuretic dosages before commencement of SGLT2i treatment
warnings.actionRequired: Assess volume status, reduce concomitant diuretic dose if needed, advise patients about symptoms of volume depletion and low blood pressure, follow up on volume status after drug initiation

warnings.warningText: A reversible decrease in the eGFR with commencement of SGLT2i treatment may occur and is generally not an indication to discontinue therapy
warnings.actionRequired: Anticipate acute eGFR drop after initiating SGLT2i; this is not a reason to discontinue treatment unless >30% decline occurs

warnings.warningText: If patient is on insulin or sulfonylurea, has history of severe hypoglycemia, or HbA1c is at or below goal — risk of hypoglycemia when combined with other glucose-lowering medications
warnings.actionRequired: Educate on hypoglycemia symptoms and glycemia monitoring, consider insulin or sulfonylurea dose reduction

warnings.warningText: Withhold SGLT2i the day of day-stay procedures and limit fasting to minimum required; withhold SGLT2i at least 2 days in advance for major surgery
warnings.actionRequired: Measure both blood glucose and blood ketone levels on hospital admission; proceed with procedure/surgery if patient is clinically well and ketones <1.0 mmol/L; restart only when eating and drinking normally

warnings.warningText: Temporarily withhold SGLT2i during illness or excessive exercise or alcohol intake
warnings.affectedPopulation: Patients during illness or excessive exercise/alcohol intake
warnings.actionRequired: Keep drinking and eating if possible, check blood glucose and blood ketone levels more often, seek medical help early

warnings.warningText: If there is a greater than 30% decline in eGFR with initiation, evaluate for other causes before discontinuing
warnings.affectedPopulation: Patients with >30% eGFR decline after initiation
warnings.actionRequired: Check for hypovolemia, adjust diuretic dose, discontinue other nephrotoxic agents, and evaluate for other causes of acute decline in kidney function

warnings.warningText: Routine preventive foot care and adequate hydration are advised for all patients receiving SGLT2i
warnings.actionRequired: Provide routine preventive foot care education and ensure adequate hydration
